questions.explanation: Union contains every element belonging to A or B.

questions.text: If n(A)=18, n(B)=20, n(A∩B)=6 and n(U)=40, how many elements are outside A∪B?
Which of the following is equivalent to (A∪B)' ?
Evaluate Σ(i²) for i=1 to 5.
If Σx=96 and n=12, find the arithmetic mean.
If Σx=45, Σx²=255 and n=9, find E(X²).
How many ways can a captain and vice-captain be selected from 8 players?
How many different committees of 4 can be formed from 9 people?
If P(A)=0.7, find P(A').
If P(A)=0.5, P(B)=0.4 and P(A∩B)=0.2, find P(A|B).
Two events A and B satisfy P(A)=0.35, P(B)=0.45 and P(A∩B)=0. Which statement is TRUE?
Which of the following is NOT a valid probability distribution?
questions.explanation: Probabilities must sum to 1. Here they sum to 1.2.

questions.text: A fair die is rolled once. Let X be the number obtained. Find Var(X).
If Σx=84, Σx²=630 and n=12, find E(X).
If Σx=84, Σx²=630 and n=12, find E(X²).
Using the information in Questions 14 and 15, find Var(X).
A box contains 6 white balls and 4 black balls. Two balls are drawn without replacement. What is the probability that both are white?
If P(A)=0.6, P(B)=0.5 and P(A∩B)=0.3, find P(A∪B).
A committee of 2 men and 1 woman is to be formed from 5 men and 4 women. How many different committees are possible?
If X~Bin(6,0.5), find P(X=0).
If X~Bin(5,0.2), what is the probability of getting at least one success?
A machine has a probability 0.15 of producing a defective item. Find the probability that the first defective item occurs after the third inspection.
questions.explanation: 'After the third inspection' means the first three are non-defective.

questions.text: If X~Poisson(3), what is Var(X)?
questions.explanation: For a Poisson distribution, variance equals λ.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is FALSE?
options: Mutually exclusive events cannot occur together.
Every mutually exclusive pair of events is independent.
questions.explanation: Mutually exclusive events are generally not independent unless one has probability zero.

questions.text: Which probability distribution is most appropriate for modelling the number of customers entering a bank in 10 minutes?
questions.explanation: Poisson distribution models the number of events occurring in a fixed interval.
